title: Exposure of Internal System Information via Logs
description: Insufficiently sanitized or secured log files expose sensitive internal system information (e.g., database connection strings, internal IP addresses, API keys) to unauthorized access, potentially enabling attackers to compromise the system or gain access to other systems on the network.
attack_vector: Direct access to log files without proper security controls or encryption.
mitigations: Implement strict access controls to log files, limiting access to authorized personnel only.
Encrypt log files to protect sensitive information in transit and at rest.
Regularly review and audit log files for any sensitive information that may have been inadvertently exposed.
